metadata.title:
Y. v. P. TURNS
SUITS INTO


S|EGES

pages.raw_text:
A commission of doctors. consist
ing of Drs. P. d. Mccormack, c. S.
Kalb and Harry s. Martin, appoint
ed by Judge Sullivan on motion of
the Washington Water Power Co.,
will meet tomorrow to make an ex-
pert examination of the injuries of
Mrs. Joseph T. Koch, who is suing
the company for $1,000 damages
for personal injuries.


Mrs. Koch fell from w. w. P.
car at the corner of Riverside ave-
nue. and Browne streets last Sep
TENDER and claims to have sustain
ed injuries that have permanently
crippled her and since rendered her
unable to work at her regular call
ing, that of selling silverware at 50
per day.


The company interposed the u.s.
ual motion for an examination by
three disinterested medical ex-
perts for the purpose of enabling
the defendant to acquire informa-
tion as to the nature and extent
and probable duration of the in
juries charged to have been sus-
tanned and for the purpose of as
pertaining the physical conditions
resulting therefrom:" that such
"exact and impartial evidence" will
have great weight with the jury
and that such an examination will
promote the ends of Justice" All
such affidavits for medical exami-
nations of damages suit claimants
against the w. w. P. are usually
signed by Harry Blocker, secretary
of the company.


These medical examinations are
part of a well laid plan of legal
skirmishing by which the w. w. P.
expects to wear out persons so I'M
fortunate as to have to resort to
the courts to collect damages for
personal injuries. After the first
examination it is possible that the
company will turn up in court
few weeks later with another mo-
tion for a second medical eXamina-
ton Ito promote the ends of JUS
tice." Then failing to secure the
desired results motion for an X
ray examination will be the next
step toward securing Justice" as
viewed by the w. w. P. Co. If the
claimant is not worn out by this
time the case will then go to trial
there to face that other formidable
weapon of the corporations, under
existing laws, the motion to non
suit.
